items.scholarly_note: Foliation or page number, likely added in the medieval or early modern period.

items.region: bottom margin, below final text line
items.description: Large post-medieval inscription in darker, more cursive hand beginning with prominent 'Magnus' with long descending loops and flourishes, followed by 'Bonum' or similar; possible additional letters or name fragment to the left.
items.scholarly_note: Ownership mark, pen exercise, or annotation in a later hand; elaborate calligraphic flourishes extend across much of the lower margin.

items.description: Bright blue ink strokes crossing and marking the text, prominently over 'hljar', 'moku hofs', and the phrase 'e hljar moku hofs en mep e kosa'; includes a horizontal line through portions of the line.
items.scholarly_note: Modern (likely 19th-20th century) scholarly annotation or attempted correction; blue ink is anachronistic for the medieval period.

items.region: top left corner and upper left edge
items.description: Irregular parchment loss at the top-left corner; torn and uneven edge with missing material.
items.scholarly_note: Typical cumulative edge damage from handling, binding, and age.

items.region: bottom edge, full width with emphasis on left and center
items.description: Tattered, frayed bottom margin with multiple small tears, notches, and parchment losses.
items.scholarly_note: Consistent with wear from use and storage without modern conservation.

items.region: text block and margins, especially lower half
items.description: Multiple horizontal and diagonal creases causing cockling, surface distortion, and minor cracking of ink in folded areas.
items.scholarly_note: Parchment has been folded or pressed; creases predate current housing.

items.region: right margin, right text block, and lower right quadrant
items.description: Brownish water stains and foxing, some causing local ink bleeding and discoloration; prominent along the right edge and within the written area.
items.scholarly_note: Classic moisture damage in a vellum manuscript.

items.region: left side, mid to lower page
items.description: Dark, irregular blotch near the left margin, possibly from liquid or mold.

items.region: parchment surface, center and lower areas
items.description: Fine linear abrasions and surface scratches disturbing the vellum nap; some cross text without fully obliterating letters.
items.scholarly_note: Result of handling, rubbing, or storage contact.

items.region: main text block, lines ~14-17 and scattered elsewhere
items.description: Minor scribal corrections visible as retraced letters, slight crowding, and adjustments within words in the original hand.
items.scholarly_note: Typical in-place corrections by the main scribe of Codex Regius.

items.description: Extended decorative loops and testing strokes forming the flourishes of the large bottom inscription.
items.scholarly_note: Likely served as both decoration and practical pen/ink trial.

scratches: Fine surface abrasions across lower half and center, disturbing vellum nap.
Light linear scratches intersecting text in several places without major letter loss.
damage_notes: Significant loss of top-left corner with irregular tear.
Frayed and notched bottom edge with multiple small losses.
Creasing and cockling throughout, especially lower half, with associated ink cracking.
Right edge irregularly trimmed or torn.
Overall parchment thinning and discoloration in damaged zones.
summary: Folio shows characteristic 13th-century vellum wear: torn and tattered edges (especially top-left corner and bottom), creases, and multiple brown water/foxing stains causing some ink diffusion. Key non-original features are a large post-medieval flourished inscription 'Magnus ... Bonum' at the bottom and a conspicuous modern blue-ink annotation crossing the lower text block. Minor original scribal corrections present; no clear doodles. Text remains largely legible despite surface damage.
